title: Breakwave Dry Bulk Shipping Report 8.1.2023
summary:
Main
in Weekly Shipbuilding Reports 01/08/2023
The Atlantic Capesize market awakens, while weather in the Pacific could soon become a positive catalyst
Source: Breakwave Advisors

Linked asset: 2023-08-01_BreakwaveDryAugust12023Report.pdf
Source asset: reports/hellenic/shipbuilding/pdfs/2023-08-01_BreakwaveDryAugust12023Report.pdf

[Page 1]
BDRY
Dry Bulk Shipping
August 1, 2023
Breakwave Dry Futures Index: 1,289 Baltic Dry Index (spot): 1,127 Short-term Indicators:
↓ 30D: -2.7% ↑ 30D: 3.3% Momentum: Neutral
↑ YTD: 25.5% ↓ YTD: -25.6% Sentiment: Neutral
↓ YOY: -33.0% ↓ YOY: -40.5% Fundamentals: Positive
Bi-Weekly Report
• The Atlantic Capesize market awakens, while weather in the Pacific could soon become a positive catalyst - In
the absence of any major macro developments, the dry bulk market has recently been affected by the normal
supply-demand imbalances natural to the shipping sector, where supply of ships sometimes becomes scarce due to
scheduling issues. The Atlantic Capesize market is currently subject to such an imbalance, and as a result, rates in
that part of the world have recently moved higher, at least versus other areas. Although overall spot rates remain
well below r

Bi-Weekly Report
• The Atlantic Capesize market awakens, while weather in the Pacific could soon become a positive catalyst - In
the absence of any major macro developments, the dry bulk market has recently been affected by the normal
